verdict_reasoning: KLK2 is a serine protease of the kallikrein family, primarily expressed in prostate tissue where it is secreted and processes pro-PSA. The protein is secreted into seminal fluid and prostatic tissue; however, kallikreins including KLK2 have documented surface-anchored and/or cell-associated forms in prostate epithelium, and KLK2 upregulation in prostate cancer may involve altered trafficking or cancer-cell-restricted surface display. The protein itself is secreted and exists predominantly as a soluble protease, but prostate-tissue and cancer-context restricted localization to membrane-proximal or surface-associated pools qualifies as contextual surface accessibility.
